title: How to subtract in a spreadsheet
task: Subtract one number or cell from another — there's no SUBTRACT function, and that's by design.

excel.note: Plain minus operator. Subtract a whole range from a value with =A2-SUM(B2:B10).

google_sheets.note: Identical (Sheets also has a MINUS function, but nobody needs it).

explanation: Subtraction is just the minus operator: =A2-B2 gives 60. People search for a SUBTRACT function because SUM exists, but arithmetic operators (- * /) are the intended way. To subtract many cells from one starting value, combine with SUM: =A2-SUM(B2:B10). Dates subtract the same way — the result is the number of days between them.
